data.content: This project uses the legacy `packages.config` format, which doesn't support CPM directly. I'll migrate it to `PackageReference` format and then enable CPM.
data.reasoningText:
This is a `packages.config` project (legacy format). The CPM skill explicitly says "DO NOT USE FOR: packages.config projects (must migrate to PackageReference first)". 

So I cannot use the CPM skill here. I need to explain to the user that this project uses `packages.config` format, which must first be migrated to `PackageReference` format before CPM can be applied.

However, the user asked me to convert it to CPM. I can do this manually:
1. First migrate from packages.config to PackageReference format
2. Then set up CPM with Directory.Packages.props

Let me do this step by step:


I need to convert the old-style csproj with packages.config to an SDK-style format that uses PackageReference, then create a Directory.Packages.props file to centralize the package versions. The current project targets .NET Framework 4.8, which can work with an SDK-style csproj, so I'll proceed with the migration.

Setting up the new project structure...

toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-7eb344a472f54d3aa9110e21fb652c46/simple-packages-config/LegacyApp/LegacyApp.csproj", "old_str": "<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.8</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="Newtonsoft.Json, Version=13.0.0.0">
      <HintPath>..\packages\Newtonsoft.Json.13.0.3\lib\net45\Newtonsoft.Json.dll</HintPath>
    </Reference>
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>", "new_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net48</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>"}

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

1. **Converted `LegacyApp.csproj`** from legacy format to SDK-style with `PackageReference` (no version — managed centrally)
2. **Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and `Newtonsoft.Json` version `13.0.3`
3. **Deleted `packages.config`** (no longer needed)

The project now uses Central Package Management with the same `Newtonsoft.Json 13.0.3` dependency, just in the modern format.
